domain_context.inferred_domain: Synthetic tabular regression (Kaggle TPS Jan 2021), CTGAN-style generated from an undisclosed real source; anonymized continuous features cont1..cont14, near-normal continuous target scored by RMSE. Practical domain: generic anonymized continuous regression.

column_semantics.id: Non-predictive sequential row identifier; carries no signal, drop before modeling.
column_semantics.cont1: Anonymized continuous predictor, pre-scaled ~[-0.24,1.20] (Gaussianized by generator).
column_semantics.cont2: Anonymized continuous predictor, pre-scaled ~[-0.24,1.20]; among strongest (still weak |r|~0.067) linear correlates of target.
column_semantics.cont7: Anonymized continuous predictor, pre-scaled ~[-0.24,1.20]; single highest (|r|~0.067) linear correlation with target.
column_semantics.cont9: Anonymized continuous predictor, pre-scaled ~[-0.24,1.20]; highest skew (~0.80).
column_semantics.cont11: Anonymized continuous predictor, pre-scaled ~[-0.24,1.20]; part of strongest correlated cluster (max pairwise r~0.826).
column_semantics.cont12: Anonymized continuous predictor, pre-scaled ~[-0.24,1.20]; part of strongest correlated cluster (max pairwise r~0.826).
column_semantics.target: Continuous regression label, near-normal (range [0,10.27], mean 7.906, std 0.733, skew -0.08); scored by RMSE (lower better).

known_relationships: Linear feature-target correlations are essentially zero (max |Pearson|~0.067), so signal lives in non-linear interactions among cont features.
Some cont features are collinear (max feature-feature r~0.826) forming redundant clusters trees handle natively but that destabilize linear models.
Model hierarchy: linear/ridge floor ~0.726 RMSE; gradient-boosted trees (LightGBM/XGBoost/CatBoost) reach ~0.696; NNs competitive; blends of diverse non-linear learners win.
Features appear pre-Gaussianized, so extra scaling barely affects trees; gains come from interaction/aggregate features, not raw transforms.
